creator: dom@myrddin.demon.co.uk
text:
Here are the list of additions I would like to see to this qmail port:

.   Offer to create users/groups if they don't exist (with interactive
    warning).  Ooops, it already does this.

.   Is there a reason for changing the usernames/groupnames?  They
    differ from the docs and manpages like that, so I'm not so sure
    it's a good idea.

.   Make it go into ${PREFIX}/qmail (and set up a link to there as
    /var/qmail, for compatibility).

.   Would it be cleaner to merge that pre-configure: target in the
    Makefile to a configure script?

.   Also, moving the pre-install stuff into a script would be good.
    Ideally, I'd like to make this packageable, even if the copyright
    doesn't (at present) allow this.

.   There are lots of files (mostly qmail-1.01/[A-Z]*) that contain
    useful documentation.  These should probably be installed under
    ${PREFIX}/share/docs/qmail/. 

.   Don't install the preformatted man pages.  They'll get installed
    automatically by the man command as and when needed.  Actually, I
    can't quite see what you're doing with the man pages.

.   Ensure that it *always* gets installed on a UFS filesystem.  It's
    not a happy bunny over NFS.

.   Upgrade to 1.02.

.   Provide a /usr/local/etc/rc.d/qmail script.  This should use my
    port of supervise (from daemontools) and tcp-server (from
    ucspi-tcp) if installed, as this is the way which Dan reccomends.

.   Provide a ${PREFIX}/qmail/bin/freebsd-setup command, which takes
    care of:

    o Setting up links from /usr/sbin/sendmail to ${PREFIX}/qmail/bin/sendmail

    o Installing qmail-smtpd in /etc/inetd.conf if ucspi-tcp isn't installed.

    o Draining the sendmail queue of old messages.

Blimey.  And I thought there'd only be a couple.  :-)  If you tell me
what you think of these ideas, and then let me know which bits you
want done, I'll get right back to you.

Also, when this port has been done, I'll look at getting together some 
other ports of Dan's stuff like ezmlm, to build on it.
-- 
"Remember the Golden Rule: he who has the gold makes the rules" -- WoID
